events.label: reasoning
events.text:
We need output valid JSON with specific fields. Need decide if work meets applicable code/spec/mfr. Scenario: residential HVAC-R brazing & evacuation. Observed: vacuum not held at target microns (decay test - read the gauge, FD). Work rough-complete. Reference material: mfr specs, ACCA, EPA 608.

We need assess compliance. Vacuum decay test failure means system cannot maintain target vacuum (e.g., 500 microns), indicating leak, outgassing, moisture, non-condensables. For brazing & evacuation, EPA 608 prohibits venting refrigerants and requires proper evacuation to specified levels before charging; improper evacuation can lead to emissions. Manufacturer specs require reaching and holding target microns (often 500 microns with decay < certain). ACCA standards (ANSI/ACCA 5 QI?) maybe require evacuation verification. Thus decision fail because non-compliant and/or installed-defective. Work stage rough-complete. Component conditions: installed-defective, non-compliant. Percent complete estimate maybe 75%? Value: target microns not held, but value could be actual decay not given; maybe 0 (no numeric asked). Findings: vacuum decay test failed; system not at target microns; likely leak/moisture. Actions: locate and repair leak, replace drier, re-evacuate, re-test decay and pass, charge. Rationale. References: EPA 608 (recovery/evacuation), ACCA/ANSI standards, mfr installation instructions. Need produce raw JSON no prose. Ensure no markdown. Confidence high. Risk maybe high because contamination/moisture/leak can damage compressor and release refrigerants. Since not charged, less immediate critical? But vacuum decay indicates leak path; could allow air/moisture leading to acid/compressor failure. Risk high. Work_stage rough-complete. percent_complete maybe 80. component_conditions exactly strings from allowed: installed-defective, non-compliant. Worn/degraded/failed not necessarily. We can include installed-defective, non-compliant.

Need decide "decision": fail. percent_complete numeric estimate of overall progress: brazing/evacuation scope is rough-complete maybe 85. value: no numeric reading given? The prompt says "Use value for the numeric reading or computed quantity when the task asks for one." This task doesn't explicitly ask numeric value, but field condition vacuum not held at target microns. We can set value to 0 or null? Schema says number. Use 0 maybe. sound_source, event_time, rate not audio: empty string and 0. order empty list, workable empty list. findings list. actions list. references list. Must be valid JSON.

Let's craft concise.

Potential references: EPA 608 (Clean Air Act) Section 608; ACCA Standard 5 (HVAC Quality Installation Specification); manufacturer installation instructions. If want exact code anchors, mention "EPA 608: technician certification and proper evacuation; 40 CFR 82"; "ACCA/ANSI Standard 5 QI: system evacuation/dehydration verification"; "mfr installation manual - evacuation to target microns and decay hold".

confidence: 0.95.

Risk: high.

events.label: response
events.text:
{
  "decision": "fail",
  "risk": "high",
  "work_stage": "rough-complete",
  "component_conditions": [
    "installed-defective",
    "non-compliant"
  ],
  "percent_complete": 85,
  "value": 0,
  "sound_source": "",
  "confidence": 0.95,
  "event_time": 0,
  "rate": 0,
  "order": [],
  "workable": [],
  "findings": [
    "Vacuum decay test failed: system did not hold target microns.",
    "Evacuation is incomplete or a leak/outgassing path remains in the refrigerant circuit.",
    "System cannot be charged or placed in service until target vacuum and decay hold are achieved."
  ],
  "actions": [
    "Identify and repair the leak or contamination source.",
    "Replace the liquid-line filter-drier if opened to atmosphere or contaminated.",
    "Re-evacuate the system with a deep vacuum pump and proper manifold setup.",
    "Verify the system reaches manufacturer-specified target microns and holds the decay test.",
    "Document final micron reading and decay before charging."
  ],
  "rationale": "EPA 608, ACCA, and manufacturer instructions require the refrigerant circuit to be evacuated to a specified deep vacuum and to pass a decay (standing vacuum) test before charging. A failed decay test means the system is not dehydrated/leak-free, so the brazing/evacuation work is non-compliant and defective at the rough-complete stage.",
  "references": [
    "EPA 608 - 40 CFR Part 82: proper recovery/evacuation and prohibition against venting",
    "ACCA/ANSI Standard 5 (QI) - HVAC quality installation evacuation/dehydration verification",
    "Manufacturer installation instructions - target micron level and vacuum decay hold requirements"
  ]
}
